data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- daemon • 2026-02-23T12:32 • patrol

I am Deacon. Start patrol: run gt deacon heartbeat, then check gt hook. If no hook, create mol-deacon-patrol wisp and execute it.

content.thinking:
Deacon checking in. I need to follow the startup protocol immediately:

1. Announce: "Deacon, checking in."
2. Then IMMEDIATELY run: `bd show skogtown-wisp-wisp-0ppjv5`
3. Begin execution

But wait - I have hooked work already. Let me follow the protocol exactly.

Actually, looking more carefully at the instructions:

1. First update heartbeat: `gt deacon heartbeat`
2. Check hook: `gt hook`
3. Work hooked → RUN IT

I already have the hooked work info in the context. Let me start by updating heartbeat and checking the current step.

content.content:
No molecules in progress for deacon.

To start work on a molecule:
  bd mol pour <proto-id>      # Instantiate a molecule from template
  bd update <step-id> --status in_progress  # Claim a step

content.thinking: The patrol wisp shows all children as completed (✓). Let me check the status of the hooked work more carefully.

content.text: All steps in the current patrol wisp are already completed. Let me verify and squash this patrol, then start a new one.

content.content:
? skogtown-wisp-wisp-0ppjv5 [EPIC] · mol-deacon-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: deacon · Type: epic
Created: 2026-02-23 · Updated: 2026-02-23

DESCRIPTION
Deacon patrol cycle.

Monitors Gas Town agents (Mayor, Witnesses), processes lifecycle requests,
and handles escalations. Created as wisps (ephemeral, not synced to git).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-5e40sb: Scan agent health ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-ct5w2c: Check inbox for lifecycle requests and messages ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-j6cneb: Check for orphaned processes ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-xwih9i: Decide: loop or hand off ● P2

- `hq-mayor` - expected, mayor session
